title: hibbertia coriacea var. lanceolata
intro: Hibbertia coriacea var. lanceolata, also known as the lance-leaf Guinea flower, is a shrub in the Dilleniaceae family. It is a plant native to Australia, and its leaves and flowers have been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions. Hibbertia coriacea var. lanceolata is said to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to alleviate symptoms of fever, sore throats, and respiratory issues. It can be prepared as an infusion, tincture, or ointment, and is often used topically to soothe skin irritations and wounds.
